summary: Linked several remaining item translation units by converting six item TUs to Matching in configure.py, tightening item data/header layouts, correcting symbol visibility for local constants/callbacks, and applying targeted MWCC codegen fixes across item modules. The PR body recorded the post-merge frontier as 24 remaining item TUs and 157 remaining item functions; the decomp.dev report credited +21216 linked-code bytes, +5984 matched-code bytes, 19 new matches, and 5 further unmatched-item improvements.
what_changed: configure.py changed six item objects from NonMatching to Matching: ittaru.c, itfoxblaster.c, ityoshieggthrow.c, itclimbersstring.c, itclinkmilk.c, and itkirby_2F23.c.

config/GALE01/symbols.txt updated symbol scopes and object boundaries: several Fox blaster UnkMotion callbacks became scope:local; many item strings/floats/doubles in .rodata, .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 were renamed from global it_80xxxxxx symbols to local @NNN symbols; broad data labels were split around it_803F6D58, it_803F6D90, and it_803F6DC8.
itCharItems.h collapsed FoxBlaster repeated scalar fields into arrays: xDE4[6], xDFC[6], xE14[6], and angle[6].

itfoxblaster.c was rewritten to use the new FoxBlaster arrays, including a natural for-loop history shift in it_802ADF10 and indexed reset code in it_802AE7B8, replacing earlier scalar/unrolled field handling.

itclimbersstring.c removed placeholder/unused includes and extern zero-vector dependencies, used local Vec3 zero initializers, added small inline helpers for GObj setup and joint access, and reordered/restored cleanup, animation, matrix, and link-update routines to match.

ittaru.c added the ItemStateTable it_803F58E0 in-source, adjusted random/float expression ordering, added a small sdata_ordering helper, and changed it_802886C4 zero-vector handling to pass a Vec3 through an inline helper.

itclinkmilk.c and itkirby_2F23.c added their ItemStateTable data in-source and made small codegen-sensitive changes to child/grandchild JObj use, scale math, accessory assignment ordering, and local temporaries.

ityoshieggthrow.c reordered callbacks and helper functions, added inline effect-spawn wrappers spawn1/spawn2, and reduced stack padding in clank/collision paths to match it_2725_Logic43_Clanked and it_802B2E7C.
itkusudama.c inlined spawn helper logic at call sites with explicit null checks, velocity setup, it_80274ED8 calls, and spawned-kind recording, improving but not fully linking the TU.

itkyasarin.c, itpikachuthunder.c, and itwstar.c received smaller matching tweaks involving local variable lifetimes, PAD_STACK sizes, float rounding/casts, direct gobj field access, volatile storage, and random temporary reuse.
smart_moves: The FoxBlaster header change was the highest-leverage structural fix: once the repeated xDE4/xDFC/xE14/angle groups were modeled as arrays, it_802ADF10 could use the compiler-friendly loop that the source already suspected was idiomatic, removing the fake manual unroll.
Local zero-vector initializers in climbers string removed dependencies on exported rodata symbols it_803B86B0 and it_803B86BC; the corresponding symbols could then become local @NNN constants.

Moving ItemStateTable definitions into the matching source files gave the linker/data matcher exact table ownership for it_803F58E0, it_803F7A28, and it_803F9450 instead of relying on anonymous/global data placeholders.

The PR paired code changes with symbol-map hygiene: functions and constants that were only TU-local were marked scope:local, preventing bogus global symbol names from becoming part of the decomp surface.

Several fixes deliberately shaped MWCC variable lifetimes and evaluation order rather than changing behavior: Taru chained assignments, Kirby scale temporaries, Pikachu thunder rounding casts, Kyasarin rand temporaries, and itwstar volatile/direct field access.

For Kusudama and Yoshi egg throw, tiny wrappers or inlined call sequences were chosen based on codegen needs, showing that decomp readability sometimes has to follow the original compiler's call boundaries.
decomp_lessons: When six adjacent scalar fields are repeatedly shifted or reset in identical groups, check whether the original struct was an array; matching code often becomes simpler and more idiomatic after the header layout is corrected.
Do not keep address-named globals for constants that the linked TU proves are local. Use @NNN local symbols and scope:local in symbols.txt when data has no true external linkage.
Data matching is part of linking a TU: ItemStateTable ownership, table splitting, string/floating constant locality, and exact object sizes can matter as much as function bodies.

Function order, static-inline placement, and callback visibility can affect both .text and .data matching. Reordering source to match original layout is a valid tactic when evidence comes from the diff and symbol map.
MWCC matching often depends on local variable declaration order, stack padding, volatile qualifiers, direct gobj->hsd_obj/user_data access versus macros, and expression sequencing.
Removing placeholder includes is safest when real headers now provide the needed types/prototypes; it can also expose or enforce correct local/static symbol ownership.
For future item work, use the decomp.dev delta table to identify small remaining near-matches: this PR converted many 95-99% functions to 100% with narrow codegen changes rather than large rewrites.
naming_conventions: Item motion callbacks follow item/module plus UnkMotionN_Anim, UnkMotionN_Phys, and UnkMotionN_Coll, e.g. itFoxblaster_UnkMotion8_Anim, itClinkmilk_UnkMotion1_Anim, and itKirby_2F23_UnkMotion0_Coll.
Item logic callbacks use LogicNN event names such as it_2725_Logic70_PickedUp, it_2725_Logic70_EvtUnk, itYoshiEggThrow_Logic43_PickedUp, and it_2725_Logic43_Clanked.
Unidentified global data tables kept address-style names such as it_803F58E0, it_803F7A28, and it_803F9450 when no semantic name was established.
Synthetic local data/constants in symbols.txt use @NNN names with scope:local; local data-section boundary labels may use ...data.0.
Unknown functions continue to use address names like it_802C248C, it_802C33B8, it_802B2E7C, and it_802886C4 until a stronger semantic name is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Convert repeated state fields to arrays and use indexed assignment/loops when the assembly shows strided history movement, as in FoxBlaster xDE4/xDFC/xE14/angle.
Use local Vec3 zero = { 0.0F, 0.0F, 0.0F } or pass a stack Vec3 into an inline helper to avoid unwanted rodata references and match stack layout.
Tune PAD_STACK and explicit padding locals, including PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(12), PAD_STACK(32), and u8 _pad arrays, to match caller/callee frame shapes.

Preserve expression ordering with temporaries and chained assignments: examples include Taru random angle setup, Kirby scale calculation, Pikachu thunder fixed-point-ish rounding, and Kyasarin HSD_Randi temporaries.
Use volatile storage when needed to pin stack/register behavior, as in itwstar's saved_xDA4.
Prefer direct gobj->hsd_obj and gobj->user_data access over GET_JOBJ/GET_ITEM macros when the resulting codegen matches better.
Use small static inline wrappers for repeated setup only when they inline to the desired call sequence, such as it_802C248C_setupGObj and Yoshi egg spawn1/spawn2.
Inline formerly shared helper logic at call sites when wrapper boundaries cause mismatches, as in the Kusudama spawn loops.
Adjust symbol visibility in tandem with source changes: changing functions/constants from global address names to local @NNN/scope:local is part of achieving a clean linked TU.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.

The only recorded feedback was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code 65.40% (+0.15%, +5984 bytes), linked code 37.87% (+0.55%, +21216 bytes), matched data 38.67% (+0.07%, +864 bytes), and linked data 32.98% (+0.09%, +1120 bytes).

The bot reported 19 new matches and 5 improvements in still-unmatched items; notable new matches included .data for ittaru, itclinkmilk, and itkirby_2F23, it_802C248C in itclimbersstring, it_802ADF10 in itfoxblaster, and Yoshi egg throw clank/collision functions.
The PR body recorded remaining item work after this slice as 24 item TUs and 157 item functions.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining item TUs still marked Object(NonMatching) after PR 2442 and compare against the array/layout and local-symbol tactics used here.
Search item headers for repeated scalar field groups like FoxBlaster xDE4/xDFC/xE14/angle that could be represented as arrays.
Audit config/GALE01/symbols.txt for item .rodata/.sdata/.sdata2 constants still exported as it_80xxxxxx globals but likely local @NNN symbols.
Revisit the partially improved functions in itkusudama, itkyasarin, itpikachuthunder, and itwstar using PR 2442 tactics: variable lifetime, PAD_STACK, volatile locals, and helper inlining.
Search matching item TUs for placeholder includes that can be removed once real headers and local declarations are sufficient.
Inspect data tables near it_803F6D58/it_803F6D90/it_803F6DC8 for other broad lbl_ data symbols that should be split into exact ItemStateTable-sized objects.
